text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY

------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
SPECIMEN ID: SP-XXXX-XXXX
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Head and Neck Surgery / Otolaryngology
CLINICAL INDICATION: HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, right base of tongue (T2N1M0, stage I by HPV-positive AJCC 8th ed. staging criteria), status post definitive concurrent cisplatin-based chemoradiation (70 Gy IMRT, 6 of 7 planned weekly cisplatin cycles). Surveillance biopsy: residual 0.9 cm lesion at right base of tongue on post-treatment MRI; direct laryngoscopy performed under general anesthesia with narrow-band imaging (NBI) for tissue sampling.
PROCEDURE: Direct laryngoscopy with biopsy under general anesthesia, narrow-band imaging
SURGEON OF RECORD: Attending, Head and Neck Surgery Service
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC ASSIGNMENT]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC ASSIGNMENT]
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Right base of tongue, biopsy (3 fragments)
  B. Right lateral pharyngeal wall, biopsy (2 fragments)
  C. Right tonsillar fossa (residual cuff), biopsy (2 fragments)
  D. Hypopharynx, biopsy (2 fragments)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. RIGHT BASE OF TONGUE, BIOPSY:
   - EXTENSIVE RADIATION EFFECT WITH STROMAL FIBROSIS, VASCULAR ECTASIA, AND RADIATION-INDUCED ATYPIA OF STROMAL CELLS.
   - NO VIABLE CARCINOMA IDENTIFIED.
   - No residual squamous cell carcinoma. No dysplasia. No necrosis attributable to viable tumor.
   See comment.

B. RIGHT LATERAL PHARYNGEAL WALL, BIOPSY:
   - SQUAMOUS MUCOSA WITH REACTIVE EPITHELIAL CHANGES.
   - NO MALIGNANCY.
   - No viable carcinoma. No high-grade dysplasia.

C. RIGHT TONSILLAR FOSSA (RESIDUAL CUFF), BIOPSY:
   - BENIGN LYMPHOID TISSUE WITH POST-RADIATION FIBROSIS AND REACTIVE CHANGES.
   - NO MALIGNANCY.
   - No viable carcinoma. No dysplasia.

D. HYPOPHARYNX, BIOPSY:
   - SQUAMOUS MUCOSA, WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS.
   - NO MALIGNANCY.
   - No carcinoma. No dysplasia.

OVERALL CONCLUSION: NO VIABLE SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA IDENTIFIED IN ANY OF THE FOUR BIOPSY SITES. FINDINGS ARE CONSISTENT WITH COMPLETE PATHOLOGIC RESPONSE TO DEFINITIVE CONCURRENT CHEMORADIATION.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (right base of tongue) demonstrates the characteristic histopathologic features of radiation-induced tissue injury. Squamous epithelium overlying the biopsy site shows moderate epithelial reactive atypia with nuclear enlargement, occasional multinucleation, and prominent nucleoli; however, the chromatin pattern is smudgy and degenerative rather than hyperchromatic and mitotically active, and there is no architectural distortion or stromal invasion to suggest viable carcinoma. The lamina propria is markedly expanded by dense collagenous fibrosis with hyalinization consistent with post-radiation stromal remodeling. Dilated, ectatic blood vessels with thickened, fibrinoid walls are prominent throughout the dermis and submucosa, consistent with radiation-induced vascular injury. Scattered large, bizarre stromal cells with irregular hyperchromatic nuclei and abundant cytoplasm are noted; these are consistent with radiation-induced fibroblastic atypia (radiation fibroblasts) and are NOT interpreted as residual or recurrent carcinoma given the absence of keratinization, intercellular bridges, invasive growth, or desmoplastic stromal response. Scattered chronic inflammatory infiltrate (predominantly lymphocytes and plasma cells) is present. No ghost cells, keratin pearls, or necrotic tumor nests to suggest prior viable squamous carcinoma are identified on routine H&E sections. Mucicarmine stain: negative (no mucin production). p16 immunohistochemistry performed on specimen A: focal weak cytoplasmic staining only in scattered inflammatory and reactive stromal cells; no diffuse block-like nuclear/cytoplasmic staining in any epithelial population, which would be required to interpret as HPV-related. This pattern is not consistent with residual HPV-associated carcinoma (see Ancillary Studies).

Specimens B, C, and D demonstrate reactive mucosal changes within the radiation field, including mild epithelial reactive atypia, submucosal edema, and vascular ectasia. Specimen C (right tonsillar fossa) contains small residual lymphoid aggregates with preserved germinal center architecture admixed with extensive fibrous replacement; no atypical lymphoid population or carcinoma is identified. No perineural invasion, lymphovascular invasion, or pathologic lymph node tissue is identified in any specimen.

Correlation with concurrent intraoperative findings (narrow-band imaging: smooth post-radiation mucosa, no mucosal irregularity or NBI-positive vascular atypia at right base of tongue) and post-treatment imaging (PET-CT: complete metabolic response; MRI: 0.9 cm residual fibrotic lesion without pathologic enhancement) is recommended and is consistent with the interpretation of complete pathologic response.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Specimen A, Right Base of Tongue):

  p16 (CINtec, clone E6H4): Focal weak cytoplasmic positivity in scattered stromal/inflammatory cells only. NO diffuse block-like nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in any squamous epithelial population. Interpretation: NOT consistent with transcriptionally active HPV infection in residual carcinoma. This pattern is distinctly different from the diagnostic p16 IHC result at initial diagnosis (diffusely and strongly positive, >75% block-like staining). NEGATIVE for HPV-associated carcinoma.

  CK5/6 (D5/16B4): Positive in residual overlying squamous epithelium; negative in atypical stromal cells. Interpretation: confirms squamous differentiation of the surface epithelium; atypical stromal cells are of non-epithelial (fibroblastic/myofibroblastic) lineage, consistent with radiation fibroblasts.

  p63 (4A4): Positive in basal/parabasal cells of squamous epithelium; negative in atypical stromal cells. No p63-positive invasive cell nests identified in the stroma.

  Ki-67 (MIB-1): Low proliferative index in squamous epithelium (~5-8%). No mitotic activity identified in atypical stromal cells. Interpretation: low proliferative activity inconsistent with viable carcinoma (prior diagnostic biopsy showed Ki-67 of approximately 65%).

HPV RNA IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (Specimen A, Right Base of Tongue):
  HPV RNA ISH (RNAscope, high-risk HPV types 16/18/31/33/35/39/45/51/52/56/58/66/68): NEGATIVE. No punctate or diffuse HPV RNA signal identified in any epithelial cell population. Interpretation: No transcriptionally active HPV infection identified in residual tissue. This is consistent with the absence of viable HPV-associated carcinoma and with the expected loss of HPV signal following definitive chemoradiation-induced tumor clearance.

  Note: HPV RNA ISH result is pending final quality control verification and will be confirmed in an addendum if discordant. Preliminary result: NEGATIVE.

ADDITIONAL STAINS (Specimen A):
  Mucicarmine: Negative (no intracytoplasmic mucin).
  PAS-D: Negative for fungal organisms. Mild PAS-positive basement membrane thickening noted, consistent with radiation change.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right Base of Tongue, Biopsy): Received fresh, then placed in 10% neutral buffered formalin. Specimen consists of three irregular tan-white to tan-pink tissue fragments, each ranging from 0.2 cm to 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. Aggregate tissue: 0.5 x 0.4 x 0.2 cm. Fragments are firm with a rubbery consistency; no discrete nodule or grossly visible tumor mass is identified. No necrosis grossly. All tissue submitted in one block (A1).

Specimen B (Right Lateral Pharyngeal Wall, Biopsy): Two irregular tan-pink tissue fragments measuring 0.3 cm and 0.4 cm in greatest dimension. Aggregate: 0.4 x 0.3 x 0.2 cm. Mucosa appears smooth. All tissue submitted in one block (B1).

Specimen C (Right Tonsillar Fossa, Residual Cuff, Biopsy): Two tan-pink to tan-gray tissue fragments measuring 0.4 cm and 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. Aggregate: 0.5 x 0.4 x 0.3 cm. Fragments have a slightly firmer consistency than tonsillar tissue, consistent with post-radiation fibrosis. No grossly identifiable nodule or mass. All tissue submitted in one block (C1).

Specimen D (Hypopharynx, Biopsy): Two tan-pink mucosal fragments measuring 0.2 cm and 0.3 cm in greatest dimension. Aggregate: 0.3 x 0.2 x 0.1 cm. No grossly identifiable lesion. All tissue submitted in one block (D1).

Total number of blocks submitted: 4 (A1, B1, C1, D1).
Number of H&E slides examined: 4 (one per block, 4 micron sections).
Levels examined: 3 levels per block (A1: levels 1-3; B1: levels 1-3; C1: levels 1-3; D1: levels 1-3).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen A (Right Base of Tongue): Sections show stratified squamous epithelium with focal mild reactive atypia. No carcinoma in situ or invasive carcinoma. The submucosa demonstrates dense collagenous fibrosis with hyalinized stroma, markedly ectatic blood vessels with fibrinoid wall thickening and endothelial prominence, and scattered large radiation-type fibroblasts with cytomegaly, nuclear pleomorphism, and smudgy chromatin -- all classic features of prior radiation effect. A chronic inflammatory infiltrate of lymphocytes and plasma cells is present in the submucosa. There is no desmoplastic stromal response, no invasive cell nests, no perineural invasion, no lymphovascular invasion, and no keratin pearls to indicate viable squamous carcinoma. Overlying epithelium demonstrates intact polarity and progressive maturation.

Specimen B (Right Lateral Pharyngeal Wall): Non-keratinizing stratified squamous mucosa with mild reactive epithelial changes including nuclear enlargement and occasional binucleation. Submucosa with mild edema, scattered chronic inflammation, and mild fibrosis. No invasive carcinoma, no dysplasia.

Specimen C (Right Tonsillar Fossa): Small islands of lymphoid tissue with preserved follicular architecture surrounded by extensive collagenous fibrosis. The overlying squamous epithelium shows mild reactive atypia consistent with radiation effect. No lymphoepithelial lesions, no carcinoma in situ, no invasive carcinoma. The degree of fibrous replacement exceeds what would be expected from tonsillectomy effect alone and is attributable to prior radiation therapy.

Specimen D (Hypopharynx): Non-keratinizing squamous mucosa with normal polarity and maturation. Submucosa unremarkable. No atypia, no carcinoma, no dysplasia.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SYNCHRONOUS/PREVIOUS PATHOLOGY CORRELATION:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Comparison to prior diagnostic pathology (direct laryngoscopy biopsy at initial diagnosis): The initial biopsy demonstrated invasive squamous cell carcinoma, moderately differentiated, with diffuse strong p16 positivity (block-like staining, >75% of tumor cells), CK5/6 positive, p63 positive, Ki-67 ~65%, and HPV RNA ISH positive for high-risk HPV type 16. The current specimens show no histopathologic, immunohistochemical, or molecular evidence of residual or recurrent carcinoma. The complete absence of p16 block-like staining and negative HPV RNA ISH in the current specimens, combined with absent viable tumor morphology, supports a complete pathologic response to definitive chemoradiation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist, Head and Neck Surgical Pathology]
SIGNED OUT: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC ASSIGNMENT]

Addendum note: HPV RNA ISH final quality control confirmed -- result NEGATIVE, consistent with preliminary report. No additional addendum required.
------------------------------------------------------------------------
